notes:
XY base set (xy1), released Feb 2014, 146 cards, the FIRST set of the XY era. Booster = 10 cards: 1 rare-or-better slot, 3 uncommon, ~5 common, 1 reverse-holo (+ basic Energy, not modeled). Box = 36 packs, ETB = 8 packs.

SET COMPOSITION (codedyellow.com card list): NO secret rares (set ends at 146/146, no cards above). Poke EV buckets given (ultra-rare:14, rare-holo:16, rare:26, uncommon:42, common:48) have no secret-rare/ex bucket, so Poke EV has merged ALL 14 EX cards (8 half-art Rare Holo EX + 6 full-art EX, cards 141-146: Venusaur/M Venusaur/Blastoise/M Blastoise/Yveltal/Xerneas/Skarmory/Emolga EX) into the 'ultra-rare' bucket. rare-holo:16 = non-EX holo rares; rare:26 = plain rares.

PULL RATES (Flipside Gaming Part Two): XY-era pull rates 'started out matching those found in Plasma Blast' (xy1 is the very first XY set, so it uses these leaner starting rates, NOT the later Ancient Origins 1:6 EX / 1:12 UR rates). Plasma Blast rates per source: Pokemon-EX ~1:18 packs, Ultra Rare / Full Art ~1:36 packs. Since Poke EV merges both half-art EX (1:18) and full-art EX (1:36) into one 'ultra-rare' bucket, I summed them: 1/18 + 1/36 = 3/36 = 0.0833 of packs yield any EX. No secret rares in this set, so no secret-rare contribution (consistent with integrity rules — not inventing a bucket the set lacks).

RARE SLOT (1/pack, resolves to rare / rare-holo / ultra-rare): ultra-rare 0.0833 (combined EX, sourced). Remaining 0.9167 split between plain rare and holo rare using the long-standing ~1:3 holo convention (Bulbapedia/PokeBeach: holo rare ~1 in 3 packs since early era): rare-holo 0.30, rare 0.6167. Sums to 1.0.

REVERSE-HOLO SLOT (1/pack): Poke EV has no reverse-holo rarity bucket and reverse holos in this era can be any common/uncommon/rare, so modeled as drawing from common/uncommon/rare pools weighted by available counts (48/42/26 = 116 total): common 0.4138, uncommon 0.3621, rare 0.2241. Sums to 1.0.

UNCOMMON slot (3/pack) and COMMON slots (4/pack) modeled at count 3 and 4 respectively.

Confidence = LOW: no per-set XY1 published pull-rate study exists; figures are era-average Plasma-Blast-start rates (Flipside, derived from community Reddit case data) applied to xy1, plus the standard ~1:3 holo convention. This is the correct confidence tier per the integrity rules (era-average rates without a per-set study). Vintage sealed XY1 boxes carry a large collector premium, so modeled EV/booster will sit well below sealed box price — expected, not a reason to inflate rates.
